steps.title: Use the discriminant for exactly one solution
steps.content: A quadratic $ax^2 + bx + \text{constant} = 0$ has exactly one solution when the discriminant equals zero. For $-9x^2 + 30x + c = 0$, we have $a = -9$, $b = 30$, and the constant term is $c$ (the value we need to find).

steps.title: Solve for c using the discriminant condition
steps.content: Set the discriminant equal to zero and substitute: $$30^2 - 4(-9)(c) = 0$$ $$900 + 36c = 0$$ $$36c = -900$$ $$c = -25$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.
